title: Promising New Treatment for Deadly E. coli Infections Demonstrated

title: Green vegetables, fermented foods and whole grains: What Americans can learn from the Tanzanian heritage diet
summary: Eating heritage foods from Tanzania’s Kilimanjaro area comes with a slew of health benefits. Adding them to your diet is easier than you might think.

title: Researchers develop nasal spray H5N1 avian influenza vaccine
summary: The State Key Laboratory for Emerging Infectious Diseases at the University of Hong Kong (HKU) and the InnoHK Center for Virology, Vaccinology and Therapeutics (CVVT) have pioneered an influenza virus vector-based nasal spray vaccine platform and developed a nasal spray H5N1 avian influenza vaccine. The study is published in Nature Communications.

title: Cognitive decline comes sooner for people with heart failure
summary: There are over six million Americans with heart failure who are at greater risk of losing their cognitive abilities earlier in life, a study suggests.

title: Cats reveal hidden danger of microplastics to unborn life
summary: Microplastics have been found in unborn cats, proving that plastic pollution can reach fetal tissues early in pregnancy.

title: Smartphones shed light on depression detection
summary: Smartphones shed light on depression detection-

title: Study finds one in five US adults use multiple drugs, revealing new patterns in substance use
summary: A study from researchers at the University of Colorado Anschutz Medical Campus reveals that nearly 1 in 5 U.S. adults have used multiple drugs in the past year, showing that substance use is fairly common and more complex than just using one drug at a time. The study was published last week in the American Journal of Public Health.

title: Parkinson's protein clumps in heart nerves could offer new treatment path
summary: Parkinson's doesn't just affect movement and the brain—it may also impact the heart, according to new research from the University of Surrey. Scientists from Surrey's School of Veterinary Medicine suggest that targeting a key protein outside of the brain could help manage Parkinson's-related heart issues.

title: Shingles vaccine cuts dementia risk by 20%, study finds
summary: A vaccine to fight dementia? It turns out there may already be one – shots that prevent painful shingles also appear to protect aging brains.

title: Earlier diagnosis and better care needed for patients with myalgic encephalomyelitis and long COVID
summary: Significant improvement in the diagnosis and care of myalgic encephalomyelitis (ME) and long COVID is urgently needed across NHS and social care services, according to a new report.
